title: 200 Art Styles for Stable Diffusion SDXL: AI's Ultimate Creative Showcase
description:
🎨 Welcome to an unprecedented journey through the world of art with AI! In this video, we're diving into over 200 art styles using Stable Diffusion SDXL, a cutting-edge AI technology. From classic Impressionism to modern Digital Art, from the grandeur of Baroque to the intriguing world of Biopunk, we're showcasing a vast array of artistic expressions.

🖥️ Technology Used:

- Stable Diffusion Automatic 1111 on Windows
- Realism Engine SDXL Checkpoints from Civit AI
- Sampling Method: DPM++ 2S a (30 steps)
Image Resolution: 1280x720 pixels
Fixed Seed: 2024
Upscaling: 4x Ultra Sharp (Denoise strength: 0.3, Upscaled by 2)

 Video Highlights:

- Each artwork is created using the prompt "portrait of a warrior woman" without negative prompts.
- Watch as we transform this single concept into more than 200 unique styles.
- Discover how each art style impacts the final image, offering a photo-realistic or stylized appearance.
- Experience a rapid showcase of styles, with a blend of well-known an
transcript: Want to see how each art style holds up in the digital era?  Join me as I put stable diffusion to the test,  exploring an array of styles from impressionism to digital art,  Baroque to Biopunk.  I am showcasing over 200 unique styles  through the lens of advanced technology.  Get ready for a journey through art history,  one digital masterpiece at a time.  I am using stable diffusion automatic 1111  installed on windows.  For the checkpoint, I am using the Realism Engine SDXL,  which is available for free from CYVID AI.  After downloading it, you place it in the model's stable diffusion  folder of your web UI.  For this test, I am using the prompt portrait  of a warrior woman without any negative prompts.  The sampling method is DPM plus plus 2SA  with 30 sampling steps.  I aim to create 2K images for this video,  so I am using a size of 1,280 by 720 pixels.  A fixed seed of 2024 is set to observe  the influence of the art style on the final image.  Now, I've enabled hires fixed, but it's important to note  that this consumes a lot of video RAM.  For upscaling, I use the Forex Ultra-Sharp Upscaler.  The Denoise Strength is set at 0.3 and it's upscaled by 2.  However, you can use a lower value like 1.5  if your PC can't handle it.  After hitting Generate, you'll see the results of not  specifying an art style in the prompt.  Each model is different.  For this one, I get a photo-realistic look on my PC generation  took 59 seconds.  For the test, I will add different art styles  to the prompt and generate images.  I will test a variety of art styles, some well-known,  and others less familiar or not typically considered  for testing.  Here is the name of the art style,  which you should add at the end of your prompt.  Accompanying it is a short description  of what that style represents.  Since I have over 200 styles, I'll add some music  and speed up the display of all the styles a bit.  Feel free to pause the video or take a screenshot  of any style you like.  Let the games begin.
